name: Trail of the Sanctuaries from Bolaños de Calatrava
description: This easy 15.4-mile touring cycling route around Bolaños de Calatrava offers varied terrain and glimpses of the region's volcanic and agricu
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bolaños De Calatrava offers routes through a landscape shaped by its volcanic origin, featuring undulating roads and hills. The region is characterized by expansive agricultural plains, including vineyards and olive groves, providing scenic backdrops for rides. Cyclists can explore areas with ancient volcanic formations and tranquil lagoons, alongside historic towns and cultural landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava The most popular touring cycling route is Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores rural scenery and a local reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route connects historic towns across agricultural plains. Local touring cyclists also love the Trail of the Sanctuaries from Bolaños de Calatrava, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail leading through local countryside and cultural landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Bolaños De Calatrava is defined by volcanic landscapes, agricultural plains with vineyards, and historic towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bolaños De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Bolaños De Calatrava, offering a variety of experiences through volcanic landscapes, agricultural plains, and historic towns. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The most pleasant times for touring cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava are spring and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are moderate, making for ideal conditions to enjoy the expansive agricultural landscapes, vineyards, and olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The terrain in Bolaños De Calatrava is largely shaped by its volcanic origin, featuring undulating roads and hills. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads, often former main roads with reduced traffic, and some unpaved segments. The routes traverse scenic agricultural plains, volcanic formations, and tranquil lagoons.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy touring cycling routes in the area, perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058521863/trail-of-the-sanctuaries-from-bolanos-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Sanctuaries from Bolaños de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.4 miles (24.8 km) long and leads through local countryside and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Bolaños De Calatrava offers 4 difficult touring cycling routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14657074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de los Santos – Silo (Almagro) loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, a 62.4 km (38.8 miles) journey with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in the region?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058505797/presa-de-jabalon-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which explores rural scenery and a local reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056883816/granatula-de-calatrava-y-moral-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, connecting historic towns.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. You can cycle past the monumental &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5685079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;settlement of Valenzuela de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; with its Arab castle. The town of Bolaños de Calatrava itself features the ancient Castle of Doña Berenguela.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to explore on a touring bike?
A: Absolutely. The area's volcanic origin provides unique natural features. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;. The 'Route of the Volcanoes Campo de Calatrava' specifically highlights these geological wonders and ferruginous water springs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bolaños De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the serene agricultural plains with vineyards and olive groves, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with cultural immersion at historic sites.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous volcanic landscapes of Campo de Calatrava?
A: Yes, many routes traverse the distinctive volcanic landscapes. The region is known for areas like Sierra del Pardillo and the Hoya de Bolaños. The 'Route of the Volcanoes Campo de Calatrava' is particularly designed to showcase volcanic signs and features like the Cervera Lagoon and the volcanic maar set of Varondillo and Cerro Gordo.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route are not detailed, the routes often connect or pass through historic towns like Almagro and Valenzuela de Calatrava. These towns offer various establishments where you can find refreshments and meals, providing convenient stops during your ride.

Q: Is there public transport access to the bike routes in Bolaños De Calatrava?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads is not readily available. However, Bolaños de Calatrava and nearby towns like Almagro are generally accessible by regional bus services. It is advisable to check local transport schedules for connections to the starting points of your chosen routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the expansive agricultural plains?
A: Many routes in Bolaños De Calatrava offer stunning views of the agricultural plains. The region is characterized by vast fields of cereals, vineyards, and olive groves. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056883816/granatula-de-calatrava-y-moral-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; specifically connect historic towns across these scenic agricultural landscapes.

The Venta de Borondo is a historic inn located between Bolaños de Calatrava and Manzanares, in the province of Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha. Built in the 16th century, this inn is known for its typical period architecture, with a rectangular floor plan, two floors, a turret on one corner, and a central courtyard.

It is believed that the Venta de Borondo may have inspired Miguel de Cervantes in his work "Don Quixote de la Mancha," as these inns were common during long voyages of the time. Currently, the inn is used as a farmhouse and second home.
The sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de las Nieves is a complex of buildings around the hermitage where the Marian image of the Virgen de las Nieves is venerated.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Bolaños de Calatrava — circular route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 24.8 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling and does not include stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Bolaños de Calatrava — circular route is classified as an easy touring bicycle ride. With an elevation gain of only 83 meters, it's suitable for most cyclists looking for a relaxed experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied terrain on this route. While many sections are likely paved, some parts may be unpaved, potentially featuring gravel or dirt tracks. It's advisable to be prepared for different surface conditions.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, meaning you can typically start and end at the same point within Bolaños de Calatrava. Specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, but parking is generally available in the town itself, from where you can access the route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by several notable highlights. These include the Greenway from Bolaños to the Virgen del Monte, the historical site of Venta de Borondo, and the serene Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Snows.

faqs.question: Is the Bolaños de Calatrava — circular route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for touring bicycles, dogs are generally welcome on cycling paths in Spain, especially if kept on a leash and under control. Please ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling speeds and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Bolaños de Calatrava — circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other known paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Ruta de la Fuente del Caño, the Rurta entre santuarios. Santuario de la Virden del Monte. Santuario de Ntra. Sra. de las Nieves, and the Camino de Manzanares.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy touring bicycle route, we recommend bringing water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and comfortable cycling attire. A helmet is always advised for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself doesn't explicitly list designated viewpoints, the journey through the Campo de Calatrava region offers characteristic Spanish countryside scenery, including agricultural areas and glimpses of the local volcanic landscape. The greenway section also provides pleasant views.
